tokens: Let's see, (these are pages for Station) number 8.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Getting pretty good at getting the old Earth in there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, I bet you that Rover would have climbed to the top of Stone...Right on up.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This is some machine, I'll tell you.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Can I turn your eyeball around and dust it out, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, that rock over there, the one I'm gonna sneak up on, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And before you leave the Rover, we'd like you to pull the Batt 2 Bus D circuit breaker.
We'll let you put it back in before you drive off.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, Tony, a comment on this pallet (probably referring to the tool gate at the back of the Rover).
The locking on the tool harness (means "tool carrier")...The locking ring on the pallet doesn't stay up every time.
It's slightly inconvenient, but no big deal.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm going to get a shovel for the scoop (sample).
And I thought we'd maybe...And I'll tell you what I got to do is fix up a...Start on a double (means "single") core for the CSVC.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; well, let me get these (pad) samples out of the way...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Got to get this out of the way.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, Charlie, was that DAC off?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, I got the DAC off.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Thanks for reminding me, Tony, (about the DAC).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: If you'll pan left, we'll show you the rock we're going to sneak up on.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's between us and the LM!
It's between the LM and us.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Oh, I know that; but they don't want you to open that thing (meaning the sampler) until you get right up next to the rock.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm going to get the other one...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'd like you to get up a little closer to it and face away from yourself before you open it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Right, you're filthy, as Fredo says.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We're missing the great rock hunt here.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: You're just not watching him sneak, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What are you all doing looking at the seats!?!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Conspiratorially) We're sneaking up on it, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: From way out here, just point it at me.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The first lunar Great Rock Hunt and we missed it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I leaned on the rock (laughing), and (I know) we can turn this one over (because it moved).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Did that (rock movement) disturb the surface on the other side there?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The picture will show how I disturbed it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's important that you put this second one down in an area that wasn't...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: No, we didn't disturb it at all.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To Tony) In an area that that (first sampler) didn't go in, right?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, John...(Demonstrating) John was sneaking just like this.
He really got up to it before it...It didn't even know he was coming.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, John, I'll give you a hint.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You picked some up on that one.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: He got some on one corner, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we'll have to take it that way.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That's the way the ground is.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Man, you fellas have outstanding finesse.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'd say about 20 percent of it's covered.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I think 20 percent...(Stops to listen) Okay, 20 percent of one corner is covered.
I mean, 20 percent of the whole thing is covered.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To John) These go in your rock bags, don't they?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let me take them back and put them under the seat.
I don't know where they go.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think they go in the SCBs.
Tony, do these go in the SCBs?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They'll go in the SCB that doesn't go in the SRC (meaning the Sample Return Container, or rock box, which is on the MESA at the LM).
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We've already got that one collected.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (I can't get the) top open, again.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, now we got to go get...After (sample) two, place the gnomon.
We got a cross-Sun 'after', and a down-Sun and a locator.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, that's going to be hard to get.
Don't put the dirt all over there!
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's going to be hard to get because...(Pause) I need to...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You can get a cross-Sun from over on this side.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We don't have to sneak anymore.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We don't need a UHT anymore, do we, Houston?
Are we going to need it for the ALSEP?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, it's sitting in the heat flow.
See how far you can throw that beauty.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I'll go get the down-Sun.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, at least in the vacuum, it doesn't boomerang.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (I'll) run around and get a locator.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Houston; I'm looking back at the LM.
Charlie, you can get a picture of the...I can get a picture if I put it on f/whatchacallit, and it'll show that rock is between us and the LM.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You can barely see the LM over there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, well, wait a minute, we got to get a skim (sample).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Can we skim where the pristine sample (that is, the pad sample) was?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, we'd like to skim next to it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: No, they want it right beside it, right there ...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You can't see any of that, there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, I probably got 5 millimeters (depth) on that skim.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's a pretty good little skim there.
What setting should I open this up to show you these (sampler) prints we got in the vacuum here?
(Correcting himself) I mean in the shadow?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, I'll get you a number on that.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I can hardly see in there, again.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I got to widen this area.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'll get you a little flightline of that.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, and Charlie's scoop is being taken right under the (skim sample as shown on
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And you've got about 10 minutes now, and we'd like to get that CSVC (Core Sample Vacuum Container).
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We can turn this rock over.
If you want us to get that sample in, we need an (time) extension.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, I'll go get the CSVC then.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) We'll go ahead and do the CSVC.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: John, you want to start sampling while I do that?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John, can you turn that over by yourself?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, I was going to give it a go.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Getting a sample off of it, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah; lost my whole set of bags.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: May I borrow your hammer, John?
This core...I think I might be able to push it in, but...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Joking as he takes the hammer) "Collision." Okay, we'll just do it right here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Tony, I'm 15 meters out to the left of the...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And remember not to hammer this one all the way in.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The (sample off the) top of that rock is a hard breccia, and I'm just going to throw it under your seat, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay; did you have a bag number?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Tony, that's about 7 centimeters out.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Feels good to me, too, to get that (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, that's got it all...On all three sides.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, now here's the picture to show where the top rock came out.
(Pause as John grunts and tips over the boulder) Charlie, I got it!
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: So you can not only sneak up on them, you can flip them over, huh?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Man, it looks like it's been sitting there for quite a while.
Look at that soil underneath it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Before I stomp all over it, Charlie, sneak over here and let's get some of this soil.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: A chip off the bottom and the soil will probably do it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: "A chip off the bottom." (Chuckles) I see a place where we can get a chip off the bottom, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Evidently you found the right boulder.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That was a real good boulder.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Son of a gun's been laying out here all this time.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, John, let me cap this...(Pause) Let me cap this little beauty here (meaning the core tube) before we lose it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I'm coming with the scoop.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, you want to just sort of sneak up so you don't sprinkle any dirt down in the bottom of this place (meaning the boulder imprint) where we turned it over.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's all caked looking, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let me get the soil before you start whacking.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) The bottom is glass covered, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: No, the black stuff is the glass.
That other is crystal...That's a crystalline rock.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, that part of it is.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And it looks just like an alkali flat in the cake that's under it, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And that's right from the deepest part.
(Pause) That sample is right in the middle, which happens to be the deepest penetration that boulder made.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Where are you going to whack it, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, Charlie, your SCB is open, so a rock might come out.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To John) Yeah; that's a good place.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Now you found a real rock.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Grabbing the scoop) Here, let me get it, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Can't you just pick it up with your shovel?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I didn't want to get it too dirty.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, we got you about a 4-centimeter chip.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Just get some photos of it and I think we've done it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: At least 5 millimeters, with a bluish cast to them.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Examining the fragment) It looks to me like it's a (pause) shocked rock with a lot of - and this is a guess - a lot of black glass in the fracture patterns.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, I'll hold it (next to the rock).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Wait a minute, I got to reset...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, that was about a 5-footer, Tony.
A little down-Sun, and the shovel is right where the...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Close my top on that thing, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, John, while your working on it back there, why don't you close that thermal cover to Charlie's OPS.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: "Thermal cover to"...(Stops to listen to Tony's "rear left") Yeah; okay.
Charlie, your old rear left OPS is open.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I can feel the heat leak.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We caught you with your fly open.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Can you suggest any way to close it?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let's go back to the...(Pause) to the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, I've got to close this CSVC up; take me a couple of minutes.
You could go get a sample.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we'd like you to pack up, John, if you could help Charlie.
I think we'll have to get on.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: He can't help me with it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Unless you could get...Well, you could get the top of that CSVC.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I just wanted to get...I got to plunge...(Pause) Okay, the plunger went right down, Tony.
It's about 4 centimeters from the top.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And before you stick it in there (meaning in the CSVC), could we have the core tube number?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I never would have believed it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I wouldn't have thought it, either.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I never would have believed that.
Hope it's down in far enough to lock it, though.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You'll have to pull the bottom...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (I need to) put the spade up.
(Pause) What did you do with the shovel, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I laid it over there on the bench (meaning the seats).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, that CSVC goes in the...(Pause, with some grunting) (Garbled)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: All right, Charlie, that CSVC goes in the SRC.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Before hearing Tony) That goes in the SRC, Charlie
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Looking at his cuff checklist) Back to Station 10, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, Charlie, before you get on there, we'd like the DAC on at 12 frames per second.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And if anybody told me this thing (meaning the Rover) would go up the side of that mountain, I'd have said, man, I don't believe...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And we need frame counts (as per
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay...(Stops to listen) Okay, Tony, I'm leaving with about 161.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I probably ought to change this mag.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Do you want me to change the mags, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To John) Did he say both change?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Tony, I don't have a black and white left.
(Pause) I'm going to run off a couple, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Three frames to go and I'm out.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, magazine Bravo's got something in it; a few frames.
You used about 50 on it yesterday.
And we can use magazine Delta.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let's put Bravo on John's (camera), since he doesn't shoot while he's driving.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Got to hold it back and then pop it.
Hold that; then pop her loose.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Delta (115/D) for me and Bravo (114B) for you.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Magazine Delta is working, and I'm starting with frame count number, oh, about 1.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Aw, that thing did come off, didn't it?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let me come around and help you put it on.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This thing just peeled off under the tape.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause as John turns toward the southeast) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The DAC is set at f/8 at 12 frames a second.
And I'm not going to turn it on until we start moving, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And we're going to have to move out.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, we're mounting up right now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They were looking at us with the big eye.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, John, we have a small configuration change.
We'd like both the (Rear) Steering and the Rear Powers to Bus Baker.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: See, when you...(Stops to listen) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, I'm glad that you're driving this thing.
I can't reach those buttons down there at the bottom.
You ought to get that before you get in.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, you got all Batt 2 CBs (Circuit Breakers) out, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're loading up Battery 1; Battery 2's a little warm.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: No, I can't reach it; Steering, Rear, Bus breaker (means Baker).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The Drive Power to Bus Baker; Rear Drive Power.
transcript.speaker: England
